content: Best vegetarian protein sources: Paneer (18g/100g), Tofu (17g/100g), Lentils/Dal (9g/100g cooked), Chickpeas (19g/100g), Kidney beans (24g/100g), Soy chunks (52g/100g), Greek yogurt (10g/100g), Cottage cheese (11g/100g), Quinoa (4g/100g cooked), Peanuts (26g/100g).

Best non-vegetarian protein sources: Chicken breast (31g/100g), Eggs (6g per egg), Fish/Salmon (20g/100g), Tuna (26g/100g), Shrimp (24g/100g), Turkey (29g/100g), Lean beef (26g/100g), Whey protein (24g/scoop).

Best pre-workout meals (eat 1-2 hours before): Banana with peanut butter, Oatmeal with berries, Whole wheat toast with honey, Rice cakes with almond butter, Fruit smoothie, Small portion of rice with chicken. Focus on easily digestible carbs with moderate protein. Avoid high fat and fiber before workout.

Best post-workout meals (eat within 1 hour): Protein shake with banana, Chicken with rice, Eggs with toast, Greek yogurt with fruits, Paneer with roti, Tuna sandwich. Aim for 20-40g protein and fast-digesting carbs for recovery.

Best foods for weight loss: High protein foods (keeps you full), Vegetables (low calorie, high fiber), Fruits (natural sweetness), Whole grains (sustained energy), Lean meats, Eggs, Green tea (metabolism boost), Water (hunger suppression). Avoid: sugary drinks, fried foods, processed snacks, white bread, excessive rice.

Best foods for muscle gain: Chicken breast, Eggs (whole), Milk, Paneer, Dal, Rice, Oats, Banana, Sweet potato, Peanut butter, Almonds, Whey protein. Eat in calorie surplus of 300-500 calories. Spread protein intake across 4-5 meals.

Healthy Indian breakfast options: Poha with peanuts and vegetables (300 cal), Upma with vegetables (280 cal), Idli with sambar (250 cal), Moong dal chilla (200 cal), Oats dalia with milk (350 cal), Besan chilla with vegetables (220 cal), Sprouts salad (180 cal), Ragi dosa (230 cal).

Healthy Indian snack options: Roasted chana (150 cal/cup), Sprouts chaat (120 cal), Makhana/fox nuts (100 cal/cup), Buttermilk/chaas (40 cal), Fruit chaat (100 cal), Roasted peanuts (170 cal/30g), Dhokla (150 cal/2 pieces), Grilled paneer tikka (200 cal).

Calorie counting tips: 1 roti = ~120 cal, 1 cup rice = ~200 cal, 1 egg = ~70 cal, 1 glass milk = ~150 cal, 1 tbsp oil/ghee = ~120 cal, 1 cup dal = ~180 cal, 100g chicken = ~165 cal, 100g paneer = ~265 cal. Use your palm as portion guide: palm = protein, fist = carbs, thumb = fats.

Hydration tips: Drink 35ml per kg bodyweight daily. Start day with 2 glasses water. Drink 200-300ml every 15-20 min during workout. Monitor urine color - pale yellow is ideal. Add lemon, cucumber, or mint for flavor. Coconut water is great post-workout. Avoid excessive caffeine. Reduce alcohol - it dehydrates.

Supplement guide for beginners: Whey Protein (if diet protein is insufficient, 1-2 scoops/day), Creatine Monohydrate (5g daily, most researched supplement), Vitamin D3 (if deficient, common in India), Fish Oil/Omega 3 (heart and joint health), Multivitamin (insurance policy). NOT needed if diet is balanced: BCAAs, pre-workout, fat burners, mass gainers.

Meal prep tips: Cook proteins in bulk on Sunday. Pre-cut vegetables for the week. Portion meals in containers. Keep boiled eggs ready. Prepare overnight oats. Freeze rotis in batches. Use slow cooker for dal. Prep salad jars. Keep healthy snacks accessible. Remove junk food from home.
